questions.question: What is a fractal?
questions.answer: A fractal is a geometric pattern that repeats itself at smaller scales and shows self-similarity.

questions.question: What is meant by self-similarity?
questions.answer: Self-similarity means that a shape looks similar at different scales.

questions.question: Name one famous fractal pattern.
questions.answer: The Sierpinski Triangle is a famous fractal pattern.

questions.question: What shape is used to start the Koch Snowflake?
questions.answer: The Koch Snowflake starts with an equilateral triangle.

questions.question: What is a prism?
questions.answer: A prism is a three-dimensional solid with two identical polygon bases connected by rectangular faces.

questions.question: What is a pyramid?
questions.answer: A pyramid is a solid with a polygon base and triangular faces meeting at a single vertex.

questions.question: What is a face of a solid?
questions.answer: A face is a flat surface of a three-dimensional solid.

questions.question: What is an edge of a solid?
questions.answer: An edge is a line segment where two faces of a solid meet.

questions.question: What is a vertex of a solid?
questions.answer: A vertex is a point where edges of a solid meet.

questions.question: What is a net of a solid?
questions.answer: A net is a flat pattern that can be folded to form a three-dimensional solid.

questions.question: How many faces does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has six faces.

questions.question: How many edges does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has twelve edges.

questions.question: How many vertices does a cube have?
questions.answer: A cube has eight vertices.

questions.question: What is a projection in geometry?
questions.answer: Projection is the representation of a three-dimensional object on a two-dimensional plane.

questions.question: What is meant by front view?
questions.answer: Front view shows how an object appears when viewed from the front.

questions.question: What is meant by top view?
questions.answer: Top view shows how an object appears when viewed from above.

questions.question: What is meant by side view?
questions.answer: Side view shows how an object appears when viewed from the side.

questions.question: What is an isometric drawing?
questions.answer: An isometric drawing is a method of representing three-dimensional objects on a two-dimensional surface using equal angles between axes.

questions.question: What is an isometric grid?
questions.answer: An isometric grid is a set of evenly spaced lines used to draw three-dimensional objects accurately.

questions.question: What does a shadow represent in geometry?
questions.answer: A shadow represents the projection of an object on a surface.

questions.question: Name one application of fractals.
questions.answer: Fractals are used in computer graphics to model natural objects like mountains and clouds.

questions.question: What is the purpose of projections in engineering drawings?
questions.answer: Projections help represent three-dimensional objects accurately on two-dimensional surfaces.

questions.question: Which mathematical concept explains fractal generation?
questions.answer: Fractal patterns are generated using recursion or repeated steps.

questions.question: Why are nets useful in geometry?
questions.answer: Nets help visualize and construct three-dimensional solids.

questions.question: How do fractals combine mathematics and art?
questions.answer: Fractals create visually appealing patterns using mathematical rules and repetition.
